hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'hominem' — 'homo' is nominative, the subject of 'potest'
hints.Vocative: Vocative would also be 'homo', but this is a philosophical statement, not an address
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'homine' — 'homo' is nominative here

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'homo, hominis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'homo' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'homo, hominis' is 3rd declension

hints.to place: 'to place' is 'ponere' — 'potest' comes from 'posse' (to be able)
hints.to demand: 'to demand' is 'postulare' — do not confuse with 'posse'
hints.to drink: 'to drink' is 'potare' — similar stem but different verb

hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'potuit' — 'potest' is present indicative
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'possit' — 'potest' is indicative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place) — 'potest' comes from 'posse'
hints.postulo, postulare, postulavi, postulatu: These are the parts of 'postulare' (to demand) — a different verb
hints.poto, potare, potavi, potatum: These are the parts of 'potare' (to drink) — similar stem but different verb

steps.target: to know / to come to know
choices: to know / to come to know

hints.to think: 'to think' is 'cogitare' — 'cognoscere' means to come to know
hints.to compel: 'to compel' is 'cogere' — do not confuse with 'cognoscere'
hints.to recognize: 'to recognize' is 'recognoscere' — a compound of 'cognoscere' but more specific

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative 3rd singular would be 'cognoscit' — 'cognoscere' is the infinitive
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'cognoscat' — 'cognoscere' is the infinitive complementing 'potest'
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'cognosceret' — 'cognoscere' is the infinitive

hints.cogito, cogitare, cogitavi, cogitatum: These are the parts of 'cogitare' (to think) — a different verb
hints.cogo, cogere, coegi, coactum: These are the parts of 'cogere' (to compel) — not related to 'cognoscere'
hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the parts of 'noscere' (to know) — the simple verb, but 'cognoscere' is the compound

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Deum' with -um ending is accusative
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Dei' — 'Deum' is accusative, object of 'cognoscere'

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'creaturae' — 'creaturas' with -as is accusative plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'creaturis' — 'creaturas' is accusative after 'per'
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'creaturarum' — 'creaturas' is accusative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension accusative plural ends in -os — 'creaturas' with -as is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusative plural ends in -es — 'creaturas' is 1st declension
hints.4th: 4th declension accusative plural ends in -us — 'creaturas' is 1st declension

translation: Man cannot naturally know God except through creatures.
